intro: Tetranthera diversifolia, also known as Borneo tetranthera, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Tetranthera diversifolia include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
